data.title: Ferrellgas Partners Reports Strong Start to Fiscal 2026
data.description:

The latest announcement is out from Ferrellgas Partners ( (FGPR) ).

On December 12, 2025, Ferrellgas Partners reported its financial results for the first fiscal quarter ended October 31, 2025, highlighting a strong start to fiscal 2026 with successful refinancing of notes and credit facility, and credit rating upgrades. Despite a decrease in revenue and adjusted EBITDA, the company saw increased retail sales and improved customer retention, positioning itself well for the winter season and future growth. Operational improvements and strategic initiatives are expected to drive sustainable results and add value for stakeholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (FGPR) stock is a Hold with a $19.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Ferrellgas Partners stock, see the FGPR Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on FGPR Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, FGPR is a Neutral.

Ferrellgas Partners’ overall stock score is primarily impacted by its financial challenges, including high leverage and negative profitability, which weigh heavily on the score. However, positive technical indicators and proactive corporate events provide some optimism. The valuation remains unattractive due to negative earnings and lack of dividends.

To see Spark’s full report on FGPR stock, click here.

More about Ferrellgas Partners

Ferrellgas Partners, L.P., through its operating partnership, Ferrellgas, L.P., and subsidiaries, serves propane customers in all 50 states, the District of Columbia, and Puerto Rico. Its Blue Rhino propane exchange brand is sold at over 64,000 locations nationwide. Ferrellgas employees indirectly own 1.1 million Class A Units of the partnership, through an employee stock ownership plan.

data.title: Anixa Biosciences Reports Positive Phase 1 Trial Results
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Anixa Biosciences ( (ANIX) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On December 11, 2025, Anixa Biosciences announced positive final data from its Phase 1 clinical trial of an investigational breast cancer vaccine, presented at the 2025 San Antonio Breast Cancer Symposium. The trial, conducted with Cleveland Clinic and funded by the U.S. Department of Defense, showed the vaccine met all primary endpoints, was safe, well-tolerated, and elicited immune responses in 74% of participants. These results support the advancement to Phase 2, with plans for a combination study with Keytruda in newly diagnosed breast cancer patients.

The most recent analyst rating on (ANIX) stock is a Buy with a $14.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Anixa Biosciences stock, see the ANIX Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on ANIX Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, ANIX is a Neutral.

Anixa Biosciences faces significant financial challenges, primarily due to its inability to generate revenue and persistent losses, which are compounded by negative cash flows. While its technical indicators show some positive momentum, the negative P/E ratio and lack of dividends make valuation unattractive. The company’s financial performance is the primary concern, overshadowing technical and valuation aspects.

To see Spark’s full report on ANIX stock, click here.

More about Anixa Biosciences

Anixa Biosciences, Inc. is a clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on the treatment and prevention of cancer. Its therapeutic portfolio includes an ovarian cancer immunotherapy program and a range of cancer vaccines developed in collaboration with renowned research institutions like Cleveland Clinic. Anixa’s business model involves partnering with these institutions to advance emerging technologies for commercialization.

data.description:

  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Prophase Labs ( (PRPH) ) has provided an update.

On November 30, 2025, Prophase Labs‘ stockholders approved a reverse stock split of its common stock at a ratio of one share for every ten shares, effective December 2, 2025. This move was aimed at increasing the per-share trading price to meet NASDAQ’s continued listing requirements. The reverse stock split did not alter stockholders’ percentage ownership, except for cash adjustments for fractional shares, and the company’s common stock continues to trade under the symbol ‘PRPH’.

The most recent analyst rating on (PRPH) stock is a Hold with a $0.13 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Prophase Labs stock, see the PRPH Stock Forecast page.

data.title: Sotherly Hotels Announces Special Meeting for Merger Vote
data.description:
New activity is brewing for Sotherly Hotels ( (SOHO) ). The company has submitted a Form DEFM14A to the SEC, indicating an upcoming shareholder vote. This form gives shareholders all the information they need to develop an informed vote. Read on to learn more about the proposed company action.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions

Sotherly Hotels Inc. has announced a special meeting of stockholders to be held on January 22, 2026, to vote on a proposed merger with Sparrows Nest LLC, a subsidiary of KW Kingfisher LLC. If approved, the merger will result in Sotherly Hotels being the surviving corporation, with stockholders receiving $2.25 per share in cash. The Board of Directors, following a unanimous recommendation from a special committee, has endorsed the merger, deeming it fair and in the best interests of the company and its stockholders. The outcome of the vote will significantly impact the company’s future operations and its stakeholders.

data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

An update from Willis Lease Finance ( (WLFC) ) is now available.

On December 11, 2025, Willis Lease Finance Corporation announced the pricing of $392.9 million in fixed-rate notes through its subsidiary, Willis Engine Structured Trust IX. The notes, secured by a portfolio of 47 aircraft engines and two airframes, are part of an offering expected to close on December 23, 2025, subject to customary conditions. The issuance aims to bolster the company’s financial operations, but the notes will not be registered under the Securities Act of 1933, limiting their sale to qualified institutional buyers and non-U.S. persons.

The most recent analyst rating on (WLFC) stock is a Buy with a $140.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Willis Lease Finance stock, see the WLFC Stock Forecast page.

To see Spark’s full report on WLFC stock, click here.

More about Willis Lease Finance

Willis Lease Finance Corporation (WLFC) is a leading lessor of commercial aircraft engines and a global provider of aviation services. The company leases large and regional spare commercial aircraft engines, auxiliary power units, and aircraft to airlines, aircraft engine manufacturers, and maintenance, repair, and overhaul providers worldwide. WLFC’s services are integrated with engine and aircraft trading, engine lease pools, asset management, and various end-of-life solutions for engines and aviation materials.

Recent Updates on CWT stock

California Water Service (CWT) recently experienced a price target adjustment due to several key developments. The California Public Utilities Commission granted the company’s request to delay its Cost of Capital applications, maintaining a stable return on equity and cost of debt, which provides financial predictability. Additionally, the company’s strong Q3 2025 earnings report, which exceeded expectations with an EPS of $1.03 against a consensus of 94 cents, demonstrated operational resilience. Despite these positive earnings, the price target was lowered, reflecting broader market conditions and potential concerns about future regulatory adjustments. The reauthorization of the Water Cost of Capital Mechanism, which adjusts rates based on bond index fluctuations, also adds an element of uncertainty to future financial performance.
